title: Creating and Stopping Threads in Java
description: A flowchart illustrating the two main ways to create a thread and the proper way to stop it using a flag-based approach.
relationships: Main thread can instantiate a Thread object by either implementing Runnable or extending Thread class
The custom run() method contains the thread's execution logic
A volatile boolean flag is checked within the run() method's loop to allow for safe termination
Calling interrupt() can be used to break out of waiting/sleeping states
